title: Digital Clock & Countdown timer - Final Touch

content.problemStatement:
Perfect - we have completed our Project!

Here are some additional tasks that you can try out.


Can you create a display box around the `digital_clock`. We have created one such version - check out the code in the IDE
Currently - you cannot exit the  timer or the clock - it keeps going on for every. 

Can you update the code to accept a user input to exit the `digital_clock` project?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 86400) – the number of seconds the digital clock should count down from.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N+1 lines. The i‑th line (starting from i=0) shows the time displayed after i seconds of countdown, formatted as HH:MM:SS with leading zeros. The first line corresponds to the initial time N seconds, the last line always shows 00:00:00. Separate the outputs of consecutive test cases with a single blank line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case starts from 5 seconds and counts down to 0, printing each second. A blank line separates the two test cases. The second test case has N=0, so only 00:00:00 is printed.
3661 seconds equals 1 hour, 1 minute and 1 second. The output shows the clock decreasing each second until it reaches 00:00:00. (Only the first few and the last line are shown for brevity).

solution.approach: For each test case we simply iterate from the given number of seconds N down to 0. For every value cur we convert it to hours, minutes and seconds using integer division and modulo: h = cur / 3600, m = (cur % 3600) / 60, s = cur % 60. The three components are printed with two digits each (leading zeros) separated by ':'. After finishing a test case we print a blank line unless it is the last test case. The algorithm is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·N_max) where N_max is the largest N among the test cases (worst‑case 86400).
